subject: Mathematics
chapter: Chapter 1: A Square and a Cube

key_points: Perfect squares are numbers that can be written as the product of a number multiplied by itself
Perfect cubes are numbers that can be written as the product of a number multiplied three times
Square numbers grow faster than natural numbers but slower than cube numbers
The square root of a number is the value whose square equals the given number
The cube root of a number is the value whose cube equals the given number
exam_tips: Memorize squares from 1² to 20² for quick calculations
Check if a number is a perfect square before finding its square root
Use prime factorisation when finding square roots of large numbers
